title: Wagner Delivers AustraliaГўв‚¬в„ўs First Dedicated Sustainable Aviation Fuel Blending Terminal
content: Wagner Sustainable Fuels has commenced operations at AustraliaГўв‚¬в„ўs first dedicated Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) blending terminal, partnering with Boeing and technology provider, FlyORO, to achieve the critical aviation and renewable energy milestone. The facility, which is powered by FlyOROГўв‚¬в„ўs proprietary patented technology, is not only an Australia first, but also the first SAF blending terminal co-located at an airport globally. Its modular design can scale with demand and be replicated as the SAF industry takes hold in Australia and the Asia Pacific. In addition, Wagner Sustainable Fuels has become AustraliaГўв‚¬в„ўs first RSB (Roundtable of Sustainable Biomaterials) certified SAF company. This gives customers a world-leading accounting process and guarantees chain-of-custody and SAF authenticity. Unlike conventional jet fuel, SAF is produced from renewable or non-fossil fuel sources Гўв‚¬вЂњ like municipal solid waste, agricultural waste, animal fats and vegetable oils Гўв‚¬вЂњ and significantly reduces carbon emissions over the fuelГўв‚¬в„ўs lifecycle, making it a more sustainable alternative for powering aircraft.
author: Analysts Account
